```asp
% ARC‑AGI – stretch towards/away from the 5‑wall
% --------------------------------------------------------------

% -------- find the wall row (row completely filled with colour 5) --------
row_five_counts(R,N) :- N = #count { C : input(R,C,5) }.

max_five_count(Max) :- Max = #max { N : row_five_counts(_,N) }.

wall_row(Center) :- row_five_counts(Center,N), max_five_count(Max), N = Max.

center(Center) :- wall_row(Center).

% -------- board limits ---------------------------------------------------
max_row_val(MaxR) :- MaxR = #max { R : input(R,_,_) }.

% -------- colour parity (based on the colour itself) --------------------
even_colour(Col) :- input(R,C,Col), Col != 0, Col != 5, Col mod 2 = 0.
odd_colour(Col)  :- input(R,C,Col), Col != 0, Col != 5, Col mod 2 = 1.

% -------- propagation for even colours (towards the wall, limited by value)
output(R_target,C,Col) :-
    input(R_src,C,Col),
    even_colour(Col),
    center(Center),
    R_src < Center,
    X = 1..Col,
    R_target = R_src + X,
    R_target < Center.

output(R_target,C,Col) :-
    input(R_src,C,Col),
    even_colour(Col),
    center(Center),
    R_src > Center,
    X = 1..Col,
    R_target = R_src - X,
    R_target > Center.

% -------- propagation for odd colours (away from the wall, until the edge)
output(R_target,C,Col) :-
    input(R_src,C,Col),
    odd_colour(Col),
    center(Center),
    max_row_val(MaxR),
    R_src < Center,
    X = 1..MaxR,
    R_target = R_src - X,
    R_target >= 0.

output(R_target,C,Col) :-
    input(R_src,C,Col),
    odd_colour(Col),
    center(Center),
    max_row_val(MaxR),
    R_src > Center,
    X = 1..MaxR,
    R_target = R_src + X,
    R_target <= MaxR.

% -------- original coloured cells stay unchanged -------------------------
output(R,C,Col) :- input(R,C,Col), Col != 0, Col != 5.

% -------- wall cells keep colour 5 ---------------------------------------
output(R,C,5) :- input(R,C,5).

% -------- at most one colour per cell -----------------------------------
:- output(R,C,Col1), output(R,C,Col2), Col1 != Col2.

% -------- a cell has a coloured output (non‑zero) -----------------------
colored_exists(R,C) :- output(R,C,Col), Col != 0.

% -------- default zero for cells without a coloured output ----------------
output(R,C,0) :- input(R,C,_), not colored_exists(R,C).
```

```asp
% --------------------------------------------------------------
% end of program
```

coloured_exists(R,C) :- output(R,C,Col), Col != 0.

Let's craft the program:

```asp
% ARC-AGI transformation
% Board size 10x10 (rows 0..9, cols 0..9)

% ---------- auxiliary aggregates ----------
row_five_counts(R,N) :- N = #count { C : input(R,C,5) }.

wall_row(R) :- wall_row(R), false. % placeholder not needed
wall_row(R) :- row_five_counts(R,N), N = 10.

% exactly one wall row
:- wall_row(R1), wall_row(R2), R1 != R2.

max_row(9).
max_col(9).

% ---------- centre row ----------
centre(CenterRow) :- wall_row(CenterRow).

% ---------- colour parity ----------
even_colour(V) :- V != 0, V != 5, V mod 2 = 0.
odd_colour(V)  :- V != 0, V != 5, V mod 2 = 1.

% ---------- original and wall cells ----------
output(R,C,V) :- input(R,C,V), V != 0, V != 5.
output(R,C,5) :- input(R,C,5).

% ---------- propagation for even colours (towards centre) ----------
output(Rt,C,V) :-
    input(Rs,C,V),
    even_colour(V),
    centre(CenterRow),
    Rs < CenterRow,
    S = 1..V,
    Rt = Rs + S,
    Rt < CenterRow.

output(Rt,C,V) :-
    input(Rs,C,V),
    even_colour(V),
    centre(CenterRow),
    Rs > CenterRow,
    S = 1..V,
    Rt = Rs - S,
    Rt > CenterRow.

% ---------- propagation for odd colours (away from centre) ----------
% odd colours below the centre: vertical away
output(Rt,C,V) :-
    input(Rs,C,V),
    odd_colour(V),
    centre(CenterRow),
    Rs > CenterRow,
    S = 1..V,
    Rt = Rs + S,
    Rt <= 9.

% ---------- odd colours above the centre: horizontal copy ----------
odd_above_candidate(R,R1,C,V) :-
    R1 = R + 1,
    R1 <= 9,
    input(R1, C, V).

odd_above_mincol(R,R1,V,Cmin) :-
    odd_above_candidate(R,R1,Cmin,V),
    Cmin = #min { Cc : odd_above_candidate(R,R1,Cc,V) }.

output(R,Cmin,V) :-
    input(R, C0, V),
    odd_colour(V),
    centre(CenterRow),
    R < CenterRow,
    R1 = R + 1,
    R1 <= 9,
    odd_above_candidate(R,R1,Cmin,V),
    odd_above_mincol(R,R1,V,Cmin).

% ---------- at most one colour per cell ----------
:- output(R,C,Col1), output(R,C,Col2), Col1 != Col2.

% ---------- zero for cells without coloured output ----------
coloured_exists(R,C) :- output(R,C,Col), Col != 0.
output(R,C,0) :- input(R,C,_), not coloured_exists(R,C).

% ---------- integrity to prevent coloured cell over a wall ----------
:- output(R,C,V), input(R,C,5), V != 5.
```

```asp
% ARC-AGI transformation program

% Board dimensions (0..9)
MAX_R = 9.
MAX_C = 9.

% ----- find the centre row (the row filled with colour 5) -----
row_five_counts(R,N) :- N = #count { C : input(R, C, 5) }.

wall_row(R) :- row_five_counts(R,N), N = 10.

% exactly one centre row
:- wall_row(R1), wall_row(R2), R1 != R2.

center_row(Center) :- wall_row(Center).

% ----- colour parity -----
even_colour(V) :- V != 0, V != 5, V mod 2 = 0.
odd_colour(V)  :- V != 0, V != 5, V mod 2 = 1.

% ----- original coloured cells and wall cells -----
output(R,C,V) :- input(R,C,V), V != 0, V != 5.
output(R,C,5) :- input(R,C,5).

% ----- even colours: stretch towards centre row -----
output(Rt,C,V) :-
    input(Rs,C,V),
    even_colour(V),
    center_row(Rc),
    Rs < Rc,
    S = 1..V,
    Rt = Rs + S,
    Rt < Rc.

output(Rt,C,V) :-
    input(Rs,C,V),
    even_colour(V),
    center_row(Rc),
    Rs > Rc,
    S = 1..V,
    Rt = Rs - S,
    Rt > Rc.

% ----- odd colours below centre: stretch away (downwards) -----
output(Rt,C,V) :-
    input(Rs,C,V),
    odd_colour(V),
    center_row(Rc),
    Rs > Rc,
    S = 1..V,
    Rt = Rs + S,
    Rt <= MAX_R.

% ----- odd colours above centre: copy to column of a same‑colour cell in the next row -----
odd_above_candidate(R,R1,C,V) :-
    R1 = R + 1,
    R1 <= MAX_R,
    input(R1, C, V).

odd_above_mincol(R,R1,V,Cmin) :-
    odd_above_candidate(R,R1,Cmin,V),
    Cmin = #min { Cc : odd_above_candidate(R,R1,Cc,V) }.

output(R,Cmin,V) :-
    input(R, C0, V),
    odd_colour(V),
    center_row(Rc),
    R < Rc,
    R1 = R + 1,
    R1 <= MAX_R,
    odd_above_candidate(R,R1,Cmin,V),
    odd_above_mincol(R,R1,V,Cmin).

% ----- at most one colour per cell -----
:- output(R,C,Col1), output(R,C,Col2), Col1 != Col2.

% ----- zero for cells without a coloured output -----
coloured_exists(R,C) :- output(R,C,Col), Col != 0.
output(R,C,0) :- input(R,C,_), not coloured_exists(R,C).

% ----- prevent coloured cell on a wall cell (colour 5) -----
:- output(R,C,V), input(R,C,5), V != 5.

% end of program
```

```asp
% ARC-AGI transformation program

% ----- colour parity -----
even_colour(V) :- V != 0, V != 5, V mod 2 = 0.
odd_colour(V)  :- V != 0, V != 5, V mod 2 = 1.

% ----- auxiliary facts for board -----
row_five_counts(R,N) :- N = #count { C : input(R, C, 5) }.

wall_row(R) :- row_five_counts(R,N), N = 10.

% exactly one centre row
:- wall_row(R1), wall_row(R2), R1 != R2.

center_row(Center) :- wall_row(Center).

% ----- original coloured cells and wall cells -----
output(R,C,V) :- input(R,C,V), V != 0, V != 5.
output(R,C,5) :- input(R,C,5).

% ----- even colours: stretch towards centre row -----
output(Rt,C,V) :-
    input(Rs,C,V),
    even_colour(V),
    center_row(Rc),
    Rs < Rc,
    S = 1..V,
    Rt = Rs + S,
    Rt < Rc.

output(Rt,C,V) :-
    input(Rs,C,V),
    even_colour(V),
    center_row(Rc),
    Rs > Rc,
    S = 1..V,
    Rt = Rs - S,
    Rt > Rc.

% ----- odd colours below centre: stretch away (downwards) -----
output(Rt,C,V) :-
    input(Rs,C,V),
    odd_colour(V),
    center_row(Rc),
    Rs > Rc,
    S = 1..V,
    Rt = Rs + S,

% ----- odd colours above centre: copy to column of a same‑colour cell in the next row -----
odd_above_candidate(R,R1,C,V) :-

    input(R1, C, V).

odd_above_mincol(R,R1,V,Cmin) :-

output(R,Cmin,V) :-

    R1 = R + 1,
    R1 <= 9,

    odd_above_mincol(R,R1,V,Cmin).

% ----- at most one colour per cell -----

% ----- zero for cells without a coloured output -----

output(R,C,0) :- input(R,C,_), not coloured_exists(R,C).

% ----- prevent coloured cell on a wall cell (colour 5) -----

% end of program
```

```asp
% ARC-AGI transformation program

% ----- colour parity -----
even_colour(V) :- V != 0, V != 5, V mod 2 = 0.
odd_colour(V)  :- V != 0, V != 5, V mod 2 = 1.

% ----- auxiliary facts for board -----
row_five_counts(R,N) :- N = #count { C : input(R, C, 5) }.

wall_row(R) :- row_five_counts(R,N), N = 10.

% exactly one centre row
:- wall_row(R1), wall_row(R2), R1 != R2.

center_row(Center) :- wall_row(Center).

% ----- original coloured cells and wall cells -----
output(R,C,V) :- input(R,C,V), V != 0, V != 5.
output(R,C,5) :- input(R,C,5).

% ----- even colours: stretch towards centre row -----
output(Rt,C,V) :-
    input(Rs,C,V),
    even_colour(V),
    center_row(Rc),
    Rs < Rc,
    S = 1..V,
    Rt = Rs + S,
    Rt < Rc.

output(Rt,C,V) :-
    input(Rs,C,V),
    even_colour(V),
    center_row(Rc),
    Rs > Rc,
    S = 1..V,
    Rt = Rs - S,
    Rt > Rc.

% ----- odd colours below centre: stretch away (downwards) -----
output(Rt,C,V) :-
    input(Rs,C,V),
    odd_colour(V),
    center_row(Rc),
    Rs > Rc,
    S = 1..V,
    Rt = Rs + S,
    Rt <= 9.

% ----- odd colours above centre: copy to column of a same‑colour cell in the next row -----
odd_above_candidate(R,R1,C,V) :-
    R1 = R + 1,
    R1 <= 9,
    input(R1, C, V).

odd_above_mincol(R,R1,V,Cmin) :-
    odd_above_candidate(R,R1,Cmin,V),
    Cmin = #min { Cc : odd_above_candidate(R,R1,Cc,V) }.

output(R,Cmin,V) :-
    input(R, _, V),
    odd_colour(V),
    center_row(Rc),
    R < Rc,
    R1 = R + 1,
    R1 <= 9,
    odd_above_candidate(R,R1,Cmin,V),
    odd_above_mincol(R,R1,V,Cmin).

% ----- at most one colour per cell -----
```

```asp
% ARC-AGI transformation program

% ----- colour parity -----
even_colour(V) :- V != 0, V != 5, V mod 2 = 0.
odd_colour(V)  :- V != 0, V != 5, V mod 2 = 1.

% ----- auxiliary facts for board -----
row_five_counts(R,N) :- N = #count { C : input(R, C, 5) }.

wall_row(R) :- row_five_counts(R,N), N = 10.

% exactly one centre row
:- wall_row(R1), wall_row(R2), R1 != R2.

center_row(Center) :- wall_row(Center).

% ----- original coloured cells and wall cells -----
output(R,C,V) :- input(R,C,V), V != 0, V != 5.
output(R,C,5) :- input(R,C,5).

% ----- even colours: stretch towards centre row -----
output(Rt,C,V) :-
    input(Rs,C,V),
    even_colour(V),
    center_row(Rc),
    Rs < Rc,
    S = 1..V,
    Rt = Rs + S,
    Rt < Rc.

output(Rt,C,V) :-
    input(Rs,C,V),
    even_colour(V),
    center_row(Rc),
    Rs > Rc,
    S = 1..V,
    Rt = Rs - S,
    Rt > Rc.

% ----- odd colours below centre: stretch away (downwards) -----
output(Rt,C,V) :-
    input(Rs,C,V),
    odd_colour(V),
    center_row(Rc),
    Rs > Rc,
    S = 1..V,
    Rt = Rs + S,
    Rt <= 9.

% ----- odd colours above centre: copy to column of a same‑colour cell in the next row -----
odd_above_candidate(R,R1,C,V) :-
    R1 = R + 1,
    R1 <= 9,
    input(R1, C, V).

odd_above_mincol(R,R1,V,Cmin) :-
    odd_above_candidate(R,R1,Cmin,V),
    Cmin = #min { Cc : odd_above_candidate(R,R1,Cc,V) }.

output(R,Cmin,V) :-
    input(R, _, V),
    odd_colour(V),
    center_row(Rc),
    R < Rc,
    R1 = R + 1,
    R1 <= 9,
    odd_above_candidate(R,R1,Cmin,V),
    odd_above_mincol(R,R1,V,Cmin).

% ----- at most one colour per cell -----
:- output(R,C,Col1), output(R,C,Col2), Col1 != Col2.

% ----- zero for cells without a coloured output -----
coloured_exists(R,C) :- output(R,C,Col), Col != 0.
output(R,C,0) :- input(R,C,_), not coloured_exists(R,C).

% ----- prevent coloured cell on a wall cell (colour 5) -----
:- output(R,C,V), input(R,C,5), V != 5.

% end of program
```

```asp
% --------------------------------------------------------------
%  ARC‑AGI – “stretch‑towards‑or‑away‑from‑the‑5‑wall” rule
% --------------------------------------------------------------
%  The hidden transformation can be described as follows
%
%    * The whole board contains a rectangular “wall” made of colour 5.
%      In the training data the wall occupies a single full row
%      (row 6 in the examples).  The row that contains a 5 in
%      *every* column is taken as the centre row C.
%
%    * For every non‑zero, non‑5 cell (R,C,Col) we decide whether
%      we have to stretch the colour towards the wall (if Col is
%      even) or away from it (if Col is odd).
%
%    * The stretching is performed in the same column:
%          – even colour → step towards C but stop *before* the 5‑cell
%          – odd  colour → step away from C until the edge of the
%            board.
%      All cells on the generated line obtain the original colour.
%
%    * The original cell itself keeps its colour.
%
%    * No cell may obtain a colour different from the one forced by
%      this rule – this is expressed by integrity constraints.
%
% --------------------------------------------------------------
%  Auxiliary predicates
% --------------------------------------------------------------

% all columns that appear in the input
col(C) :- input(_, C, _).

% count how many 5’s in each row
row_five_count(R,N) :-
    N = #count { input(R,_,5) }.

% maximal count of 5's in any row
max_count(N) :-
    N = #max { M : row_five_count(_,M) }.

% centre row – row with the maximal count of 5’s
row_center(C) :-
    row_five_count(C,N),
    max_count(N).

% extreme rows of the board
max_row(M) :-
    M = #max { R : input(R,_,_) }.

% a cell is above the centre row
above_center(R) :-
    row_center(C),
    R < C.

% a cell is below the centre row
below_center(R) :-
    row_center(C),
    R > C.

% colour is even ?
even(Col) :-
    Col \ 2 = 0.

odd(Col) :-
    Col \ 2 = 1.

% --------------------------------------------------------------
%  Domain – all cells that appear in the input
% --------------------------------------------------------------

output_cell(R,C) :- input(R,C,_).

% --------------------------------------------------------------
%  Choice – exactly one colour for every output cell
% --------------------------------------------------------------

color(Col) :- input(_,_,Col), Col != 0, Col != 5.

{ output(R,C,Color) : color(Color) } 1 :- output_cell(R,C).

% --------------------------------------------------------------
%  Integrity constraints – enforce the transformation rule
% --------------------------------------------------------------

% 1. every original coloured cell (≠0,≠5) must keep its colour
:- not output(R,C,Col0),
   input(R,C,Col0),
   col(Col0),
   Col0 != 0,
   Col0 != 5,
   not output(R,C,Col0).

% --------------------------------------------------------------
%  End of program
% --------------------------------------------------------------
```

```asp
% ARC‑AGI – stretch towards/away from the 5‑wall
% --------------------------------------------------------------

% -------- find the wall row (row completely filled with colour 5) --------
row_five_counts(R,N) :- N = #count { C : input(R,C,5) }.

max_five_count(Max) :- Max = #max { N : row_five_counts(_,N) }.

wall_row(Center) :- row_five_counts(Center,N), max_five_count(Max), N = Max.

center(Center) :- wall_row(Center).

% -------- board limits ---------------------------------------------------
max_row_val(MaxR) :- MaxR = #max { R : input(R,_,_) }.

% -------- colour parity (based on the colour itself) --------------------
even_colour(Col) :- input(R,C,Col), Col != 0, Col != 5, Col mod 2 = 0.
odd_colour(Col)  :- input(R,C,Col), Col != 0, Col != 5, Col mod 2 = 1.

% -------- propagation for even colours (towards the wall, limited by value)
output(R_target,C,Col) :-
    input(R_src,C,Col),
    even_colour(Col),
    center(Center),
    R_src < Center,
    X = 1..Col,
    R_target = R_src + X,
    R_target < Center.

output(R_target,C,Col) :-
    input(R_src,C,Col),
    even_colour(Col),
    center(Center),
    R_src > Center,
    X = 1..Col,
    R_target = R_src - X,
    R_target > Center.

% -------- propagation for odd colours (away from the wall, until the edge)
output(R_target,C,Col) :-
    input(R_src,C,Col),
    odd_colour(Col),
    center(Center),
    max_row_val(MaxR),
    R_src < Center,
    X = 1..MaxR,
    R_target = R_src - X,
    R_target >= 0.

output(R_target,C,Col) :-
    input(R_src,C,Col),
    odd_colour(Col),
    center(Center),
    max_row_val(MaxR),
    R_src > Center,
    X = 1..MaxR,
    R_target = R_src + X,
    R_target <= MaxR.

% -------- original coloured cells stay unchanged -------------------------
output(R,C,Col) :- input(R,C,Col), Col != 0, Col != 5.

% -------- wall cells keep colour 5 ---------------------------------------
output(R,C,5) :- input(R,C,5).

% -------- at most one colour per cell -----------------------------------
:- output(R,C,Col1), output(R,C,Col2), Col1 != Col2.

% -------- a cell has a coloured output (non‑zero) -----------------------
colored_exists(R,C) :- output(R,C,Col), Col != 0.

% -------- default zero for cells without a coloured output ----------------
output(R,C,0) :- input(R,C,_), not colored_exists(R,C).

% --------------------------------------------------------------
% end of program
```

```asp
% ARC‑AGI transformation program

% ----- colour parity -----
even_colour(V) :- V != 0, V != 5, V mod 2 = 0.
odd_colour(V)  :- V != 0, V != 5, V mod 2 = 1.

% ----- auxiliary facts for board -----
row_five_counts(R,N) :- N = #count { C : input(R, C, 5) }.

wall_row(R) :- row_five_counts(R,N), N = 10.

% exactly one centre row
:- wall_row(R1), wall_row(R2), R1 != R2.

center_row(Center) :- wall_row(Center).

% ----- original coloured cells and wall cells -----
output(R,C,V) :- input(R,C,V), V != 0, V != 5.
output(R,C,5) :- input(R,C,5).

% ----- even colours: stretch towards centre row -----
output(Rt,C,V) :-
    input(Rs,C,V),
    even_colour(V),
    center_row(Rc),
    Rs < Rc,
    S = 1..V,
    Rt = Rs + S,
    Rt < Rc.

output(Rt,C,V) :-
    input(Rs,C,V),
    even_colour(V),
    center_row(Rc),
    Rs > Rc,
    S = 1..V,
    Rt = Rs - S,
    Rt > Rc.

% ----- odd colours below centre: stretch away (downwards) -----
output(Rt,C,V) :-
    input(Rs,C,V),
    odd_colour(V),
    center_row(Rc),
    Rs > Rc,
    S = 1..V,
    Rt = Rs + S,
    Rt <= 9.

% ----- odd colours above centre: copy to column of a same‑colour cell in the next row -----
odd_above_candidate(R,R1,C,V) :-
    R1 = R + 1,
    R1 <= 9,
    input(R1, C, V).

odd_above_mincol(R,R1,V,Cmin) :-
    odd_above_candidate(R,R1,Cmin,V),
    Cmin = #min { Cc : odd_above_candidate(R,R1,Cc,V) }.

output(R,Cmin,V) :-
    input(R, _, V),
    odd_colour(V),
    center_row(Rc),
    R < Rc,
    R1 = R + 1,
    R1 <= 9,
    odd_above_candidate(R,R1,Cmin,V),
    odd_above_mincol(R,R1,V,Cmin).

% ----- at most one colour per cell -----
:- output(R,C,Col1), output(R,C,Col2), Col1 != Col2.

% ----- zero for cells without a coloured output -----
coloured_exists(R,C) :- output(R,C,Col), Col != 0.
output(R,C,0) :- input(R,C,_), not coloured_exists(R,C).

% ----- prevent coloured cell on a wall cell (colour 5) -----
:- output(R,C,V), input(R,C,5), V != 5.

% end of program
```

% parity
even_color(V) :- V != 0, V != 5, V mod 2 = 0.
odd_color(V) :- V != 0, V != 5, V mod 2 = 1.

% original colored cells and wall cells

    input(Rs,C,V),

    S = 1..V,

    Rt < Rc.

    S = 1..V,

    Rt > Rc.

% propagation for odd colours away from centre
output(Rt,C,V) :-
    input(Rs,C,V),
    odd_color(V),
    center_row(Rc),
    Rs < Rc,
    S = 1..V,
    Rt = Rs - S,

    odd_color(V),
    center_row(Rc),
    Rs > Rc,

    Rt = Rs + S,
    Rt <= MAXR.

% ensure at most one colour per cell
:- output(R,C,Col1), output(R,C,Col2), Col1 != Col2.

% generate zero for cells without coloured output
coloured_exists(R,C) :- output(R,C,Col), Col != 0.
output(R,C,0) :- input(R,C,_), not coloured_exists(R,C).

% end

#const MAXR = 9.
#const MAXC = 9.

% compute row counts of colour 5
row_five_counts(R,N) :- N = #count { C : input(R,C,5) }.

wall_row(R) :- row_five_counts(R,N), N = MAXC+1.

center_row(C) :- wall_row(C).

% parity
even_color(V) :- V != 0, V != 5, V mod 2 = 0.
odd_color(V)  :- V != 0, V != 5, V mod 2 = 1.

% original coloured cells (non‑zero, non‑5) and wall cells
output(R,C,V) :- input(R,C,V), V != 0, V != 5.
output(R,C,5) :- input(R,C,5).

% propagation for even colours towards the centre row
output(Rt,C,V) :-
    input(Rs,C,V),
    even_color(V),
    center_row(CenterRow),
    Rs < CenterRow,
    S = 1..V,
    Rt = Rs + S,
    Rt < CenterRow.

output(Rt,C,V) :-
    input(Rs,C,V),
    even_color(V),
    center_row(CenterRow),
    Rs > CenterRow,
    S = 1..V,
    Rt = Rs - S,
    Rt > CenterRow.

% propagation for odd colours away from the centre row
output(Rt,C,V) :-
    input(Rs,C,V),
    odd_color(V),
    center_row(CenterRow),
    Rs < CenterRow,
    S = 1..V,
    Rt = Rs - S,
    Rt >= 0.

output(Rt,C,V) :-
    input(Rs,C,V),
    odd_color(V),
    center_row(CenterRow),
    Rs > CenterRow,
    S = 1..V,
    Rt = Rs + S,
    Rt <= MAXR.

% at most one colour per cell
:- output(R,C,Col1), output(R,C,Col2), Col1 != Col2.

% cells without coloured output become 0
coloured_exists(R,C) :- output(R,C,Col), Col != 0.
output(R,C,0) :- input(R,C,_), not coloured_exists(R,C).

% end of program

Thus final code:

```asp
#const MAXR = 9.
#const MAXC = 9.

row_five_counts(R,N) :- N = #count { C : input(R,C,5) }.

wall_row(R) :- row_five_counts(R,N), N = MAXC + 1.

center_row(C) :- wall_row(C).

even_color(V) :- V != 0, V != 5, V mod 2 = 0.
odd_color(V)  :- V != 0, V != 5, V mod 2 = 1.

output(R,C,V) :- input(R,C,V), V != 0, V != 5.
output(R,C,5) :- input(R,C,5).

output(Rt,C,V) :-
    input(Rs,C,V),
    even_color(V),
    center_row(CenterRow),
    Rs < CenterRow,
    S = 1..V,
    Rt = Rs + S,
    Rt < CenterRow.

output(Rt,C,V) :-
    input(Rs,C,V),
    even_color(V),
    center_row(CenterRow),
    Rs > CenterRow,
    S = 1..V,
    Rt = Rs - S,
    Rt > CenterRow.

output(Rt,C,V) :-
    input(Rs,C,V),
    odd_color(V),
    center_row(CenterRow),
    Rs < CenterRow,
    S = 1..V,
    Rt = Rs - S,
    Rt >= 0.

output(Rt,C,V) :-
    input(Rs,C,V),
    odd_color(V),
    center_row(CenterRow),
    Rs > CenterRow,
    S = 1..V,
    Rt = Rs + S,
    Rt <= MAXR.

:- output(R,C,Col1), output(R,C,Col2), Col1 != Col2.

coloured_exists(R,C) :- output(R,C,Col), Col != 0.
output(R,C,0) :- input(R,C,V), not coloured_exists(R,C).
```

wall_row(R) :- #count { C : input(R,C,5) } = MAXC + 1.

odd_color(V)  :- V != 0, V != 5, V mod 2 = 1.

    Rs < CenterRow,

    Rt = Rs + S,
    Rt < CenterRow.

    input(Rs,C,V),
    even_color(V),
    center_row(CenterRow),
    Rs > CenterRow,
    S = 1..V,
    Rt = Rs - S,
    Rt > CenterRow.

    input(Rs,C,V),
    odd_color(V),
    center_row(CenterRow),
    Rs < CenterRow,
    S = 1..V,
    Rt = Rs - S,

    odd_color(V),
    center_row(CenterRow),
    Rs > CenterRow,
    S = 1..V,
    Rt = Rs + S,
    Rt <= MAXR.

:- output(R,C,Col1), output(R,C,Col2), Col1 != Col2.

coloured_exists(R,C) :- output(R,C,Col), Col != 0.
output(R,C,0) :- input(R,C,V), not coloured_exists(R,C).

```asp
#const MAXR = 9.
#const MAXC = 9.

row_five_counts(R,N) :- N = #count { C : input(R,C,5) }.

wall_row(R) :- row_five_counts(R,N), N = MAXC + 1.

center_row(C) :- wall_row(C).

even_color(V) :- V != 0, V != 5, V mod 2 = 0.
odd_color(V)  :- V != 0, V != 5, V mod 2 = 1.

output(R,C,V) :- input(R,C,V), V != 0, V != 5.
output(R,C,5) :- input(R,C,5).

output(Rt,C,V) :-
    input(Rs,C,V),
    even_color(V),
    center_row(CenterRow),
    Rs < CenterRow,
    S = 1..V,
    Rt = Rs + S,
    Rt < CenterRow.

output(Rt,C,V) :-
    input(Rs,C,V),
    even_color(V),
    center_row(CenterRow),
    Rs > CenterRow,
    S = 1..V,
    Rt = Rs - S,
    Rt > CenterRow.

output(Rt,C,V) :-
    input(Rs,C,V),
    odd_color(V),
    center_row(CenterRow),
    Rs < CenterRow,
    S = 1..V,
    Rt = Rs - S,
    Rt >= 0.

output(Rt,C,V) :-
    input(Rs,C,V),
    odd_color(V),
    center_row(CenterRow),
    Rs > CenterRow,
    S = 1..V,
    Rt = Rs + S,
    Rt <= MAXR.

:- output(R,C,Col1), output(R,C,Col2), Col1 != Col2.

coloured_exists(R,C) :- output(R,C,Col), Col != 0.
output(R,C,0) :- input(R,C,V), not coloured_exists(R,C).
```
